result.summary: Daytona exposes a REST API for sandbox/control-plane operations, a separate per-sandbox Toolbox HTTP API, a `daytona` CLI, and a local MCP server started through that CLI.
result.description: Daytona provides cloud infrastructure for running isolated sandboxes and desktop-like environments for AI agents and code execution. It lets users create, manage, and interact with persistent compute environments programmatically.

daytona_api_key.label: Daytona API key
daytona_api_key.setup: Create an account in the [Daytona Dashboard](https://app.daytona.io/), then open [API Keys](https://app.daytona.io/dashboard/keys), click **Create Key**, choose permissions/expiration, and copy the key. Use it as `DAYTONA_API_KEY` in SDKs/CLI configs or send it as `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>` to the API.

daytona_jwt.label: Daytona CLI access token (JWT)
daytona_jwt.setup: Run `daytona login` after installing the [Daytona CLI docs](https://www.daytona.io/docs/en/tools/cli.md). The CLI authenticates you and stores the access token in `config.json` under the active profile (`api.token.accessToken`). Docs state JWT-authenticated requests to API-key management endpoints must also include `X-Daytona-Organization-ID` with your organization ID.

variables.name: sandboxId
variables.description: Sandbox identifier appended after the toolbox proxy base URL; docs say the base may also come from the sandbox's `toolboxProxyUrl` field.
